title: A stale running process can mask a correct build
body:
The live Understanding app served the *old* Mermaid empty state even though the code on disk was already fixed. Root cause: the **running** harness process predated the current `bin/Release` build — so the fix was compiled but never loaded.

**Lesson:** when a deployed change "doesn't show up," check the *process start time* against the *build timestamp* before re-debugging the code. The disk can be right while memory is stale.

title: Verify UI / proxy fixes in a real browser, not just curl
body:
`curl` proves the *bytes* are served; it does not prove the *page renders*. Stale caches, service workers, and proxy sub-path traps only show up in a real browser.

**Lesson:** before claiming a UI or proxy fix works, drive it with a headless browser (Playwright) and observe behaviour — not just an HTTP status code.

title: Prove it on an isolated preview before the live swap
body:
Self-development is risky: the Repo *is* the running Harness. Building into the live `bin/` or restarting `:5099` blind can strand the operator.

**Lesson:** build to an isolated dir and run on a throwaway port with an isolated data dir first. Confirm the exact behaviour there, *then* do the disruptive swap on `:5099`.

title: The harness serves client/dist straight from the repo root
body:
`ResolveDistPath()` resolves `client/dist` relative to the repo root (`../../../../client/dist` from the exe), and static files are read per-request.

**Consequence:** a **frontend** rebuild shows up on reload with **no restart**. Only a **compiled C#** change needs the process restarted. Knowing which layer changed tells you whether a restart is even required.

title: no-store on the SPA shell defeats "I deployed but see the old version"
body: Serving `index.html` / `version.json` as `no-store` (while hashed `/assets/*` stay immutable) means a reload always lands on the latest build. The proxy also sets `no-store` on proxied HTML so a fixed product is never shadowed by a cached pre-fix page.

title: A companion Understanding-app SPA for every non-trivial explanation
body:
The convention: when I explain something non-trivial, I also *visualize* it as a small build-less SPA at `understanding-app/`. This Lab is itself an instance of the build-less local-app pattern.

**Under test:** does authoring a throwaway SPA per feature pay for itself in shared understanding, or is it overhead? Tracking which features actually got opened.

title: Plan = a root index linking one detail file per feature
body:
`plan.md` at the repo root is a thin index; each feature gets its own `plans/<feature>.md` with a status header. Active / proposed / shipped are sections in the index.

**Under test:** whether this scales as shipped features pile up (the index is already long). Candidate refinement: archive shipped entries to a separate file.

title: One feature per branch, off main
body: Every feature gets its own branch cut from `main`; features are never bundled. Merges stay atomic, history reads cleanly, and a feature can be abandoned without dragging others down.

title: No silent fallback — empty means empty
body:
The Understanding app (and now this Lab) show an **explicit empty state** when no SPA is present, and a plain **404** for any missing asset. There is deliberately no diagram/other-content fallback.

**Why it's good:** a broken or absent app is *visibly* broken and can never masquerade as working.

title: Understanding-first: restate the request before building
body: For any substantive request I write my understanding to `understanding.md` first; the Harness renders it in a panel so the operator can confirm "you understood me" before work proceeds. Cheap to write, catches misreads before they cost a build.

title: A duplicate checkout produced self-mislabeled dashboard cards
body:
Keeping two checkouts of the same repo (`birocode` + `birocode-copy`) caused **duplicate dashboard cards**, one mislabeled as the self repo.

**Fix / rule:** one canonical live checkout (`birocode`); the copy was retired. Always deploy from the canonical checkout.

title: Launching claude.cmd via cmd.exe truncated multiline prompts
body:
A chat message with newlines reached the agent only up to its **first line**: launching the npm `claude.cmd` shim routes through `cmd.exe`, which ends the command at the first newline, cutting off the `-p "<prompt>"` argument.

**Fix:** resolve to the real `claude.exe`; keep `claude.cmd` only as a last resort.
